ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for a spicy deep-fried chicken wrap in a flour tortilla. FOODON_00002764 (chicken wrap) is the closest defensible match.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken sandwich/wrap — processed, fast food category
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers chicken-based sandwiches and wraps in the processed fast food category; used as closest defensible code for a spicy chicken wrap.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for a branded fast-food spicy chicken wrap.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Spicy Chicken McWrap weighs approximately 215 g and constitutes a single fast-food meal serving.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Chicken McWrap — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Spicy Chicken McWrap (cr_id G00010012), confidence 0.95. All per-100g values are taken from the HPB record without modification.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's Spicy Chicken McWrap. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the analogous food category 'wheat flour tortilla wrap with mixed filling' from the University of Sydney GI Database. The refined wheat flour tortilla is the dominant carbohydrate driver. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × carbohydrate per serving (48.61 g from 22.61 g/100g × 215 g serving) / 100 = 29.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 562 mg/100g; a single 215 g wrap delivers approximately 1208 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this item; if consumed, pair with a low-sodium meal for the remainder of the day and replace any accompanying soft drink with water to avoid additional sodium retention.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour tortilla with estimated GI of 60 and a glycemic load of approximately 29 per serving; low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100g) provides insufficient buffering of postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this item or substitute a wholegrain wrap, request grilled rather than deep-fried chicken, and omit high-sugar sauces to reduce the glycaemic load per ADA dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high sodium (562 mg/100g), trans fat (0.2 g/100g from deep-frying), saturated fat (~2.8 g/100g), and cholesterol (42 mg/100g) presents a multi-factor cardiovascular risk profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this item due to its trans fat, saturated fat, and high sodium content; if eaten, choose a grilled chicken option and avoid accompanying fries or sugary beverages per AHA dietary recommendations.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; trans fat (0.2 g/100g) is strongly pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried preparations; request oven-baked or grilled chicken and avoid nightshade-derived chilli spices if sensitive, as these may exacerbate inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Chicken McWrap is a fast-food wrap sold by McDonald's Singapore, consisting of a deep-fried, crispy spiced chicken fillet wrapped in a soft refined wheat flour tortilla with shredded lettuce, tomato slices, and a spicy mayonnaise-based sauce. The product is classified as ultra-processed and is certified halal. Per 100 g it provides 262 kcal, 14.73 g protein, 12.44 g fat, and 22.61 g carbohydrate, with sodium at 562 mg — a high sodium load. Dietary fibre is low at 1.4 g/100g. The deep-fried preparation method generates trans fats (estimated 0.2 g/100g) and advanced glycation end-products (AGEs). The estimated glycemic index is 60 (medium), driven by the refined flour tortilla, and a standard 215 g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 29, which is clinically significant for individuals managing type 2 diabetes. The high sodium content raises concern for hypertension management, and the combination of trans fat, saturated fat, and cholesterol makes this item a high-concern food for cardiovascular disease. The wrap is a popular lunch and dinner option in Singapore's fast-food landscape and is commonly consumed as a standalone meal. Nutritionally, it provides moderate protein but is limited by low fibre, high sodium, and the pro-inflammatory effects of deep-frying. Clinicians and dietitians advising patients with cardiometabolic conditions should flag this item as a high-concern fast-food choice.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010012) with a confidence score of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. Saturated fat and trans fat values are estimated from USDA Branded Food analogues for deep-fried chicken wraps where HPB does not report these sub-fractions; this introduces minor uncertainty for those two fields only.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid sub-fraction data for this specific McDonald's Singapore product are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA Branded Food entries for equivalent products as of April 2026. trans_fat_g set to 0.2 based on USDA Branded Food analogues for deep-fried chicken wraps (source: USDA FoodData Central, Branded Food category); this is an estimate and not directly reported by HPB. saturated_fat_g set to 2.8 estimated from USDA Branded Food analogues for comparable deep-fried chicken wraps; HPB does not report saturated fat for this item. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields is not available in the HPB Foodo database for this item as of April 2026. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on wheat flour tortilla wrap category from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for McDonald's Spicy Chicken McWrap has been published. The health_suitability notes in the input record referenced a carbonated soft drink and French fries as part of a meal set; these are not part of the standalone wrap record and have not been incorporated into the per-100g nutrient values, which reflect the wrap only as reported by HPB.
